data.title: Galaxy Researcher Highlights WLFI's Recent Blacklisting Activity
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Galaxy researcher Zack shared on social media that WLFI has blacklisted 272 addresses over the past week. On-chain records indicate that some of these actions were part of a bulk blacklisting operation.

data.title: A whale sold 10,000 ETH through Wintermute, making a profit of $960,000
data.description: According to crypto analyst Ember (@EmberCN), a whale/institution sold 10,000 ETH at $4,431 via Wintermute in the past hour, acquiring 44.31 million USDC. This ETH was originally purchased at $4,335 on August 26th, resulting in a profit of approximately $960,000, bringing the cumulative ETH swing profit to $73.96 million.

data.title: Trader James Wynn's 25x ETH long position was completely liquidated
data.description: According to OnchainLens monitoring, trader James Wynn (@JamesWynnReal) suffered full liquidation in his ETH 25x leveraged long position, losing approximately $100,250.

data.title: Whale Closes ETH Long Position Following Nonfarm Data
data.description: According to BlockBeats, a cryptocurrency whale known for selling HYPE and subsequently going long on Ethereum (ETH) has recently closed their entire long position. This decision was made following the release of nonfarm payroll data, which prompted the whale to adjust their strategy. The whale closed their position approximately 15 minutes ago at a price of around $4,265 per ETH, resulting in a loss of $10.67 million on their 52,800 ETH long position.

data.title: NEAR Protocol CEO's Social Media Account Compromised
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, NEAR Protocol has issued a warning regarding the compromise of the X account belonging to NEAR One's CEO, identified as @BowenWang18. The account was reportedly hacked by external individuals and used to disseminate false information about a $1 million NEAR giveaway.

The NEAR team is actively working to restore the account's security to prevent any further risks. They plan to release an official update once full control is regained. In the meantime, users are advised not to engage with or share the fraudulent post and to remain vigilant.

data.title: Bitcoin Volatility Surges Amid U.S. Jobs Data, Gold Hits Record High
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Bitcoin experienced significant volatility as U.S. jobs data released on Friday fell short of expectations, leading to a sharp decline in its value. The cryptocurrency reached new highs of $113,400 for September before plummeting nearly $3,000 within an hour. The disappointing nonfarm payrolls report revealed that the U.S. economy added only 22,000 jobs in August, far below the anticipated 75,000. This unexpected result caused the U.S. dollar to weaken, while gold surged to new all-time highs.

Market participants are now anticipating a potential interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve at its upcoming meeting on September 17, as indicated by data from CME Group’s FedWatch Tool. The Kobeissi Letter highlighted that this jobs report is the second lowest since July 2021, signaling a rapid deterioration in the labor market. Additionally, previous months' job numbers have been revised downward, with June's figures turning negative and a loss of 357,000 full-time jobs in August.

Despite the implications of the jobs data for Bitcoin, its price action remained subdued, failing to capitalize on the situation. Traders are focusing on key resistance levels that need to be converted into support, with the 200-period simple and exponential moving averages on four-hour charts being crucial indicators. These levels have acted as resistance in recent weeks and are currently being tested again. Some traders believe reclaiming these levels could lead to further upside, while others maintain a bearish outlook, predicting a potential drop towards $100,000 or even lower to the $92,000-$94,000 CME gap level.

The cryptocurrency market remains uncertain, with mixed opinions on Bitcoin's future trajectory. Investors and traders are advised to conduct thorough research and exercise caution, as every investment carries inherent risks. This article does not offer investment advice or recommendations.

data.title: White House economic adviser Hassett: Expect the Fed to discuss a larger rate cut
data.description: White House economic adviser Hassett said the Federal Reserve will need to consider its next policy action and is expected to discuss a larger interest rate cut.
